examples.title: Frame Allocation Simulator - Equal and Proportional
examples.description: Demonstrates equal and proportional frame allocation algorithms
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows two basic frame allocation strategies. Equal allocation gives each process the same number of frames. Proportional allocation considers process size for fairer distribution.

examples.title: Page Fault Frequency Adjustment
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PFF_FrameAdjustment
INPUT: processList, upperThreshold, lowerThreshold
OUTPUT: adjusted frame allocation

INITIALIZE: each process gets minFrames

REPEAT EVERY monitoringInterval:
  FOR EACH p IN processList:
    currentPFF = p.pageFaults / p.instructionsExecuted
    
    IF currentPFF > upperThreshold THEN
      // Too many faults - allocate more frames
      p.allocatedFrames = p.allocatedFrames + framesToAdd
    
    ELSE IF currentPFF < lowerThreshold THEN
      // Too few faults - reclaim excess frames
      p.allocatedFrames = p.allocatedFrames - framesToRemove
      IF p.allocatedFrames < minFrames THEN
        p.allocatedFrames = minFrames
    
    // Reset counters for next interval
    p.pageFaults = 0
    p.instructionsExecuted = 0

  // If any frames freed, add to available pool
  availableFrames = totalFrames - SUM(all allocatedFrames)
  
RETURN adjusted allocations
examples.explanation: This algorithm implements Page Fault Frequency control. It monitors page fault rates and dynamically adjusts frame allocation. High fault rates cause more frames to be allocated; low rates cause frames to be reclaimed for other processes.

examples.title: Working Set Frame Calculator
examples.description: Calculates working set and determines required frames
examples.explanation: This algorithm simulates the working set model by analyzing page reference patterns. It identifies how many distinct pages are referenced within a time window, representing the minimum frames needed to avoid thrashing.
